_meta.source: DSIRE (NC Clean Energy Technology Center) + state energy office .gov pages + IRS
_meta.scope: Pilot 4 states (TN/WA/UT/AL) + ALL federal, manually verified against .gov primary sources
_meta.notes: Pivot from GSC 0-click crisis: 189 queries / 0 clicks 3-month — TN/WA/UT/AL incentives queries dominated. Each row source-anchored to .gov; honest 'not available' / 'phased out' notes preserved per HCU E-E-A-T.

ALL.name: Federal Solar Investment Tax Credit (ITC)
ALL.description: Federal Residential Clean Energy Credit (IRS Section 25D) equal to 30% of qualified solar PV property installed at a primary residence. Eligible costs include panels, installation labor, on-site preparation, and wiring. Public-utility rebates and manufacturer rebates reduce the qualified basis; net-metering credits do not. Available at 30% through 2032 per the Inflation Reduction Act, stepping down to 26% in 2033 and 22% in 2034. Nonrefundable, but excess carries forward.
ALL.expiration: 30% through 2032; 26% in 2033; 22% in 2034. Verify current-year percentage with IRS before filing.

ALL.name: Modified Accelerated Cost-Recovery System (MACRS) — commercial only
ALL.description: MACRS lets solar PV used in a trade or business depreciate over 5 years. Does NOT apply to a homeowner's primary residence — listed for completeness for mixed-use properties.

TN.name: Tennessee State Energy Office — Residential Solar resources
TN.description: TN.gov State Energy Office publishes residential solar guidance, federal-credit explainers, and links to TVA programs. Tennessee does NOT operate a state-level residential solar tax credit or rebate.

TN.name: TVA Tennessee Valley Renewable Energy programs
TN.description: TVA's Tennessee Valley Renewable Energy initiative coordinates residential solar guidance, the TN Valley Solar Calculator, and connections to local-utility programs across TVA territory. Direct payments depend on the local distributor utility.

TN.name: Nashville Electric Service — Music City Solar (community solar)
TN.description: NES community-solar program for residential customers in Nashville/Davidson County who cannot install rooftop solar. Pay a monthly subscription for a share of off-site solar production credited on the electric bill.

TN.name: Middle Tennessee Electric — Cooperative Solar
TN.description: Cooperative-solar program for MTEMC member households. Subscribers receive bill credits for production from a shared solar facility.

TN.name: Knoxville Utilities Board — KUB Community Solar
TN.description: KUB's community-solar program for Knoxville-area residential customers. Subscribe to a share of off-site solar capacity and receive bill credits proportional to production.

TN.name: TN State-mandated net metering — NOT available
TN.description: Tennessee does not have a statewide net-metering requirement. Whether excess generation is credited (and at what rate) depends entirely on the local distributor utility. Confirm interconnection terms with your utility before signing a solar contract.

WA.name: Washington Sales and Use Tax Exemption (solar 1-100 kW AC)
WA.value: 100% exemption on equipment + qualifying installation labor
WA.description: Sales and use tax does not apply to purchases or installation of solar PV systems generating at least 1 kW and at most 100 kW AC. Exemption covers machinery, equipment, and labor when installed by a registered contractor with current licenses, industrial insurance, and a clean wage-law record (24 months). Buyer presents WA DOR Buyer's Retail Sales Tax Exemption Certificate at purchase.

WA.name: Washington net-metering requirement
WA.value: Excess generation credited at retail rate
WA.description: Washington Utilities and Transportation Commission (UTC) requires utilities to net-meter customer-generated electricity. Excess kWh produced and exported are credited at the utility's retail electricity rate. Carryover and true-up rules vary by utility.

WA.name: Washington Solar for All (low-income)
WA.description: WA Department of Commerce administers the federally funded Solar for All program targeting low- and moderate-income households. Direct subsidy or rebate value depends on enrollment cohort and household income.

WA.name: Renewable Energy System Incentive Program (RESIP) — closed
WA.description: Washington's RESIP production-incentive program reached its statutory cap and has been fully subscribed since June 2021. New residential applicants are NOT eligible. Existing participants continue to receive payments through their committed term.

WA.name: Washington property tax — solar treatment
WA.description: Washington does not have a blanket property-tax exemption for residential solar. Real-property reassessment treatment for added solar value varies by county assessor. Check with your county assessor before installation.

UT.name: Utah Renewable Energy Systems Tax Credit — residential solar PV ENDED 2024
UT.description: Utah's state Residential Renewable Energy Systems Tax Credit is NOT available for solar PV systems installed in 2024 or later. The credit was historically 25% of system cost, capped at $1,600 (2018-2020), $1,200 (2021), $800 (2022), $400 (2023). Per HB 264 (2025 session) the remaining commercial credit fully expires after Jan 1, 2028 — residential PV phase-out is already complete.

UT.name: Rocky Mountain Power Net Energy Metering (NEM) — pre-Nov 2017 grandfathered
UT.description: If your interconnection application was complete before Nov 15, 2017, you remain a NEM customer through Dec 31, 2035. Energy is netted monthly at the retail rate; excess production carries forward as ¢/kWh credit.
UT.expiration: Through Dec 31, 2035 for grandfathered customers; closed to new applicants

UT.name: Rocky Mountain Power Export Credit (Post-Transition customers)
UT.description: If your interconnection application was complete after Oct 30, 2020, exported customer-generated energy is compensated at fixed seasonal export rates rather than retail-rate net metering. Significantly less favorable than legacy NEM.

UT.name: Utah Residential Solar Energy Consumer Protection Act (Title 13 Ch 52)
UT.description: Utah law mandates installer disclosures, written contracts with required terms, and consumer-cancellation windows for residential solar installations. Not a financial incentive but a buyer-protection layer when comparing installer quotes.

UT.name: Utah Office of Consumer Services — Rooftop Solar guidance
UT.description: Utah OCS publishes consumer-facing rooftop-solar guidance: how to evaluate quotes, common contract pitfalls, and links to PSC tariffs. Free resource.

AL.name: Alabama State Energy Program (SEP)
AL.value: Energy-efficiency programs, not direct solar rebate
AL.description: ADECA administers Alabama's State Energy Program funded by the U.S. DOE. Focus is broader energy efficiency, weatherization, and building codes. Does NOT directly rebate residential solar PV installation costs but supports related efficiency upgrades.

AL.name: Alabama IRA Home Energy Rebate Programs (pending)
AL.value: Pending federal approval and program launch
AL.description: ADECA is implementing Inflation Reduction Act Home Energy Rebate Programs covering home electrification and energy-efficiency upgrades. As of early 2026, residential PV is NOT a primary rebate target — focus is heat pumps, induction stoves, weatherization. Confirm latest status with ADECA before relying on this program.

AL.name: Alabama state tax credit — NOT available
AL.description: Alabama does not offer a state income-tax credit for residential solar PV installation. The Alabama Department of Revenue's solar guidance addresses generation-facility sales/use tax for utility-scale projects, not homeowner credits.

AL.name: TVA territory programs (Northern Alabama)
AL.description: Northern Alabama is largely served by TVA-distributor utilities (e.g., Huntsville Utilities, Decatur Utilities). TVA discontinued the Green Power Providers production-incentive program in 2019; current solar offerings depend on the local distributor utility. Contact your utility before installation.

AL.name: Alabama Power Solar (Southern AL — Alabama Power)
AL.value: Capacity reservation and interconnection fees apply
AL.description: Customers of Alabama Power (Southern Company subsidiary) may install rooftop solar but face a monthly capacity-reservation charge based on system size, plus interconnection fees. This significantly extends payback compared to TVA territory or out-of-state. Confirm current tariff with Alabama Power before signing.

AL.name: AL state-mandated net metering — NOT available
AL.description: Alabama has no state-mandated net-metering policy. Treatment of excess solar generation is set by each utility's tariff (TVA distributors, Alabama Power, or municipal). Read the interconnection agreement carefully — payback economics depend heavily on this.
